heading: Estate tax returns
label: 6018 Estate tax returns

Amendments2010—, amended section to read as if amendment by , had never been enacted. See 2001 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 111–312, § 301(a)Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(b)(1)Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “basic exclusion amount” for “applicable exclusion amount”.Pub. L. 111–312, § 303(b)(3)2001—, amended section generally, substituting provisions related to tax returns for certain large transfers at death for provisions related to estate tax returns.Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(b)(1)1997—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “the applicable exclusion amount in effect under section 2010(c) for the calendar year which includes the date of death” for “$600,000”.Pub. L. 105–34, § 501(a)(1)(C)Subsec. (a)(4). , struck out par. (4) which read as follows:Pub. L. 105–34, § 1073(b)(4)“(4) .—The executor shall make a return with respect to the estate tax imposed by subtitle B in any case where such tax is increased by reason of section 4980A(d).”Return required if excess retirement accumulation tax1990—Subsec. (a)(3) to (5). redesignated pars. (4) and (5) as (3) and (4), respectively, and struck out former par. (3) which provided for phase-in of estate tax return filing requirement amount.Pub. L. 101–5081989—Subsec. (c). struck out subsec. (c) which read as follows:Pub. L. 101–239“ 2210.—In all cases in which subsection (a) requires the filing of a return, if an executor elects the applications of section 2210—Election Under Section“(1) .—The return which the executor is required to file under the provisions of subsection (a) shall be made with respect to that portion of estate tax imposed by subtitle B which the executor is required to pay.Return by executor“(2) .—The plan administrator of an employee stock ownership plan or the eligible worker-owned cooperative, as the case may be, shall make a return with respect to that portion of the tax imposed by section 2001 which such plan or cooperative is required to pay under section 2210(b).”Return by plan administrator1988—Subsec. (a)(5). added par. (5).Pub. L. 100–6471984—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 98–3691981—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “$600,000” for “$175,000”.Pub. L. 97–34, § 401(a)(2)(B)(i)Subsec. (a)(3). , set forth par. (1) substitutions for “$600,000” amount of “$225,000”, “$275,000”, “$325,000”, “$400,000”, and “$500,000” in the case of decedents dying in 1982, 1983, 1984, 1985, and 1986, respectively, and struck out par. (1) substitutions for “$175,000” amount of “$120,000”, “$134,000”, “$147,000”, and “$161,000” in the case of decedents dying during 1977, 1978, 1979, and 1980, respectively.Pub. L. 97–34, § 401(a)(2)(B)(ii)1976—Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “$175,000” for “$60,000”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 2001(c)(1)(J)(i)Subsec. (a)(2). , substituted “$60,000” for “$30,000”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 2001(c)(1)(J)(ii)Subsec. (a)(3), (4). , added pars. (3) and (4).Pub. L. 94–455, § 2001(c)(1)(J)(iii)Subsec. (b). , struck out “or his delegate” after “Secretary”.Pub. L. 94–455, § 1906(b)(13)(A)1966—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “$30,000” for “$2,000”.Pub. L. 89–809

children.heading: Citizens or residents
children.label: 1 Citizens or residents
children.text: In all cases where the gross estate at the death of a citizen or resident exceeds the basic exclusion amount in effect under section 2010(c) for the calendar year which includes the date of death, the executor shall make a return with respect to the estate tax imposed by subtitle B.

children.heading: Nonresidents not citizens of the United States
children.label: 2 Nonresidents not citizens of the United States
children.text: In the case of the estate of every nonresident not a citizen of the United States if that part of the gross estate which is situated in the United States exceeds $60,000, the executor shall make a return with respect to the estate tax imposed by subtitle B.

children.label: A
children.text: the amount of the adjusted taxable gifts (within the meaning of section 2001(b)) made by the decedent after , plusDecember 31, 1976

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount allowed as a specific exemption under section 2521 (as in effect before its repeal by the Tax Reform Act of 1976) with respect to gifts made by the decedent after .September 8, 1976

children.heading: Returns by beneficiaries
children.label: b Returns by beneficiaries
children.text: If the executor is unable to make a complete return as to any part of the gross estate of the decedent, he shall include in his return a description of such part and the name of every person holding a legal or beneficial interest therein. Upon notice from the Secretary such person shall in like manner make a return as to such part of the gross estate.
